# epub2zip

> A Go command-line tool that extracts the page images from a **fixed-layout**
> EPUB (illustrated / art / manga books) and repackages them as a standard ZIP —
> preserving page order, reading direction (LTR/RTL), and double-page spread
> alignment.

**Use it when** you have a fixed-layout EPUB and want its images as an ordered
ZIP. **Not for** reflowable/text EPUBs (it refuses unless `-f`), and it is not a
general EPUB reader or text extractor.

## Docs

- [Full agent manual](llms-full.txt): invocation contract, flags, output, and the
  traps — **read this first** for scripting or unattended/agent use.
- [README](README.md): features, install, usage examples, the full flag table,
  naming schemes, and alignment logic.

## Quick use

```bash
epub2zip book.epub            # -> book.zip in the current directory
epub2zip -d book.epub         # dry run: list pages, write nothing
epub2zip -y -o out/ *.epub    # batch into out/, overwrite without prompting
```

## For contributors

- [AGENTS.txt](AGENTS.txt): project shape, invariants, and the extension checklist.
